subject: Sports
title: The Science of Reaction Time
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Science of Reaction Time.

hook.text: A tennis serve takes 0.4 seconds to reach the receiver. The brain needs 0.2 seconds just to react.

buildup.text: Reaction time is the delay between stimulus and response—the brain must detect, process, and send a signal.

discovery.text: Elite athletes don't have faster nerves. They read cues earlier—body position, ball angle, opponent patterns.

twist.text: In sprinting, a reaction time under 0.1 seconds is a false start—humans can't genuinely react that fast.

climax.text: Training anticipation, not raw reflexes, is what separates professional athletes from amateurs.

punchline.text: The best athletes don't react faster—they predict sooner.

quiz.question: How do elite athletes seem to react so quickly?
options: They read anticipatory cues earlier than amateurs
They have genetically faster nerve signals
They have smaller brains that process information faster
